text: To set effective project goals, use the SMART framework: Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of "improve customer service," aim to "reduce response time to under 2 hours by June." Break your goals into milestones, such as completing a training phase or launching a feedback system. These milestones help you track progress toward your key deliverables, like a revised support manual or a new software tool. Always align goals with stakeholders—the team, managers, or clients—to ensure everyone agrees on priorities and success criteria. This alignment prevents confusion and keeps the project focused.
